text:
The Ansem coin proves that this rotation is still early.

No doubt pumpfun and its army of scem launchers will try their best to mount comebacks, but this trend won’t reverse meaningfully.
On the eve of our most important product launch to date no less

Discretionary traders will always be idols in crypto.

Which is understandable in a sense. 

We’re talking about ppl who in theory are doing things that anyone with an internet connection can do.

That makes them relatable.

But the problem is we never hold these traders accountable. 

If their shills are wrong most of the time, they’re still given a pass.

And if they’re only right because they convinced their followers to give them a better exit, then they’re not even good traders, they’re just PnDers.

Which is why Prediction Market traders are so much cooler. Their predictions aren’t vague, and they can’t dupe the greater fool as easily.

You can’t sell a dream out of a Yes/No question then dump on your followers as easily as you can a meme.

Plus, there’s limited upside, which is an important feature of prediction markets.

If you’re late to realize the likelihood of an event happening, the entry you get and the payout you can expect are exactly proportional to the odds you see.

There’s no “we’re still so early - be my exit liquidity” nonsense.

We should normalize pressing ppl like Ansem to trade prediction markets instead if they’re actually so skilled at trading and not just using their followers as exit liquidity.

I guarantee you he won’t accept this challenge. Cuz him and most other “capital slingers” are terrible at discretionary trading. 

But it’s okay if you suck at trading, you can create markets instead and earn a sustainable stream of fees.

And now that we can have prediction markets on anything, we can separate the skilled from the scammer.

The stars of future cycles won’t be memecoin shills.

The stars will all be prediction market traders and creators.

RT @Trueo_: US <> Iran never sign peace deal and engage in hostilities after every US trading session

Polymarket: https://t.co/kzE6VURCL3

Event contracts are the closest thing to “fun” since NFTs.

Memecoins were never fun. They were fun to the extent that slot machines are fun.

Event contracts take you down a rabbit hole in the real world, they force you to think critically about how the future may unfold.

For a lot of ppl predicting the future is very hard. For most ppl actually. 

But where ppl aren’t the best at forecasting what’s ahead, they may be able to ask thought provoking questions.

Some ppl predict. Others ask. Collectively they let you peer into the future.

If @Trueo_ succeeds, crypto will become fun again.

I know this sounds like a shameless shill, and that’s because it is.

But it’s also because I truly believe it.

We don’t have KOLs. We don’t have any VC backers. We have a very small community. 

But we have a massive vision.

And that’s all we really need.

Over the years it’s been proven that protocol incentives cannot conflict with token holders if the system is to work.

The token has to benefit from the incentives otherwise it will fail, and along with it, your most valuable marketing tool.

So when we launch our rewards, you best believe that TRUE will be central to the system, and potentially the main beneficiary.

Solano is like Cardano except with actual working scams instead of non-working scams

This is makes it an attractive KOL magnet which is why it continues to be relevant despite having proven that no serious person uses the chain
Imagine the future crash out by mining company execs when bitcoin block rewards inevitably stop covering their costs

RT @DAdvisoor: Free Roman.

Immediately.

Writing code is not a crime.

According to their "logic", every weapon manufacturer since the daw…

From the outside looking in it’s hard for users to recognize just how underdeveloped Prediction Market infra still is.

Don’t let the dozen Polymarket clones and candy shop betting sites fool you.

The rotation is still very early.
RT @boldleonidas: https://t.co/0DPArQ2lXx

I’m like 99% sure that all of MetaMask’s shortcomings can be chalked up to their product team being US based.

US based product teams are allergic to using their own onchain products for some reason.

On a side note, I’m surprised YouTube hasn’t released an AI tool for finding and embedding clips from their videos into everywhere that their link previews are supported.

Like “find the clip of Lex Friedman and Vitalik discussing BCH” and boom the clip preview is ready in a URL.

Honestly I do lean into this narrative from time to time, and it can be effective particularly because @Trueo_ is coming off such a small base while Polymarket is a behemoth.

But sometimes I think back to a very old Vitalik podcast (I think with Lex Friedman?) where V talks about how the BitcoinCash community ended being very good at criticizing Bitcoin, but not very good at actually executing on alternative solutions.

That statement stuck with me even all these years later.

I don’t want my work to be all about criticizing another project with out actually giving users a proper alternative.

So I’ve tamed back my rhetoric a bit. 

I still see all the Poly dramas and controversies, and I get DMs all the time from ppl pointing out how poorly they seem to be managing their massive success.

At this point I’m laser focused on execution.

User created markets. Liquidity. Bullet proof Oracle. And more liquidity.

Those are my priorities.

Talk is cheap. 

Trueo.

The final ingredient that’s missing for PMs to properly supplant launchpads is user created markets.

Right now anyone can bet on an existing event contract, but they can’t simply create one with proper assurances.

In Philip Tetlock’s book Superforcasting, he has names for both the ppl that make good predictions and for the ppl that ask the right questions.

A forecaster is a Fox.

A person who asks the right questions is a Hedgehog.

Hedgehogs are important because they ask questions others don’t. Even if they can’t accurately predict outcomes themselves, they know when a certain question matters.

And that’s 50% of the prediction market game.

@Trueo_ is where Hedgehogs can finally shine. Where they’re rewarded for asking the right questions at the right time.

…even if they don’t know the answer.

The trenches are in the early innings of a long term memecoin to prediction market rotation.

Prediction markets are right in all the ways that memecoins are wrong.

Memecoins rely on greater fools, prediction markets only need ppl to disagree.

Memecoins promise lottery tickets, prediction markets estimate your chances of winning up front.

New Memecoins are cannibalistic and dilutive, new prediction markets are sustainably constant.

For memecoins, being early is a prerequisite to making money, for prediction markets the only requirement is consistency.

The reason this rotation isn’t occurring faster is because onchain trading infra has been tailored towards token trading over the past 6 years. 

Despite the growing size of the PM economy, there’s next to no infra for dependency based tokens (e.g. outcome tokens), or for the dependencies themselves (oracles).

To give you an idea, there is a directory full of 100s of Uniswap V4 hooks, but all of them are for trading tokens.

For @Trueo_ we had to build a prediction market hook from scratch. Not a single other PM hook existed.

For oracles, there were maybe 2 other options. We had to build our own oracle from scratch as well.

The conditional token framework for outcome tokens only supports erc1155s, so we needed to build a new binary system for erc20s.

But as users begin rotating from circle jerk memes into PMs, the capital and attention and builders will too.

The current prediction market meta isn’t a meta, it’s a permaCycle.

A one way rotation from a highly unsustainable game of musical chairs, into a positive sum game that scales past self referentiality. 

Are prediction markets harder to build than launch pads? Yes.

Do trading PMs require more brain cells? Affirmative.

Is upside more limited in a PM than a moonshot? Yes, but that’s also why extractors can’t suck out unbounded amounts of money from late buyers.

The rotation will not slow down and soon all the big infra players will begin trying to service this new economy.

I’m certain of it.

If you don’t believe me, why don’t we bet on it? 

On @Trueo_.
RT @boldleonidas: https://t.co/8U8nfaq6YO

Question that frequently pops when I’m pitching users on @Trueo_:

“Why don’t you have rewards? Every other PM has rewards?”

A: We always planned to have incentives, but we didn’t want to do an airdrop program until our main product was live and we had a proper differentiator.

June 29th that product will go live.

And the much anticipated TRUE flywheel will kick off.

RT @Trueo_: Does the biggest IPO ever start the party, or ends it?

AT&T Wireless, 2000. record IPO → dot-com bust. Blackstone, 2007. recor…

Every “permissionless” PM creation app that I’ve seen so far is a gimmick or simply an after thought.

None of them let you create a proper prediction market that can be taken seriously and that can actually scale.

We put a lot of work into User Created Markets for @Trueo_, and it represents the first time that users can actually create legit prediction markets.

No bs. No gimmicks.
